channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: LinkedIn Exposed: The Rise of AI-Generated Content
item.description: Discover how AI is taking over LinkedIn, with 54% of long-length posts showing signs of AI usage. Learn about the platform's role in fueling this trend and what it means for users.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Noam Shazeer is back at Google, and this time he’s aiming for AGI
item.description: Noam Shazeer, one of the inventors of the transformer models that caused the current AI boom, is now leading the charge at Google toward artificial general intelligence (that is, AI that’s generally smarter than human beings).
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Cyber Monday shoppers expected to set a record on the year's biggest day for online shopping
item.description: Consumers in the United States are scouring the internet for online deals as they look to take advantage of the post-Thanksgiving shopping marathon with Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Delaware judge reaffirms ruling that invalidated massive Tesla pay package for Elon Musk
item.description: A Delaware judge has reaffirmed her ruling that Tesla must revoke Elon Musk’s multibillion-dollar pay package.
item.dc:creator: Randall Chase, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FTC opens Microsoft antitrust investigation that Trump administration must carry on or drop
item.description: Antitrust enforcers with the Federal Trade Commission have opened a wide-ranging investigation into Microsoft's business practices, starting a big legal project that an incoming Trump administration must take up or abandon.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: It looks like Apple’s forthcoming M5 chips are all about AI
item.description: What processors will be running the Apple bargains we’ll be seeking to purchase on Cyber Monday 2025? Presumably, Apple’s M5 chips will be inside some of them, with work already under way on the next amazing Apple Silicon processor.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Pat Gelsinger Steps Down: What's Next for Intel?
item.description: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger departs, leaving industry experts speculating on the future of the company.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why 5G is both flexible and secure
item.description: By enabling dynamic VPNs through the regular network locally or nationwide, 5G supports secure and high-priority traffic. The post Why 5G is both flexible and secure first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Helpful Hack: Siri's new tricks, photo magic and writing help with iOS 18.1's new AI features
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how to update your iPhone to add Apple Intelligence, which he says can transform your interactions with your phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Canadian news outlets sue OpenAI over copyright infringement
item.description: Five leading Canadian news outlets filed a lawsuit against OpenAI on Friday, accusing the ChatGPT owner of violating copyright laws to train its artificial intelligence (AI) models.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Australian Parliament bans social media for under-16s with world-first law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A social media ban for children under 16 passed the Australian Parliament on Friday in a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's antitrust watchdog said Thursday it is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its ad tech services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Australia passes first-ever social media ban for people under 16
item.description: Australia has passed a first-ever social media ban for youth, barring children under the age of 16 from using the platforms. The Australian Senate passed the legislation Thursday night with bipartisan backing.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Uber branches out into AI data labeling
item.description: Uber no longer offers just rides and deliveries: It’s created a new division hiring out gig workers to help enterprises with some of their AI model development work.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Australian Senate debates social media ban for under-16s
item.description: The major parties' support for the ban all but guarantees the legislation will become law. But many child welfare and mental health advocates are concerned about unintended consequences.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Uno Platform unveils visual designer for cross-platform .NET development
item.description: Uno Platform has unveiled the Uno Platform Studio, featuring a suite of developer productivity tools including Hot Design, a visual designer for building cross-platform .NET apps.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 10+ tips and services for new Bluesky users
item.description: For the past two or three weeks, upstart social network Bluesky has been attracting large numbers of new users every day, a crowd that quite evidently includes many Apple device users — the app has at times topped Apple’s App Store charts in the run-up to Thanksgiving in the US.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Gov. Newsom sets himself up as a foil to Elon Musk and Donald Trump
item.description: (Bloomberg) -- As Elon Musk prepares to wield more power in Washington, the state where he built his fortune is girding for a fight.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s House of Representatives on Wednesday passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years old from social media, leaving it to the Senate to finalize the world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal prosecutors seek records from company that deployed AI weapons scanner on NYC subway
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal investigators in New York are seeking records from the manufacturer of an AI-powered weapons scanner that was briefly deployed this summer in New York City’s subway system.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pentagon drafts large-scale government AI contract
item.description: Potential bidders have a little under a month to offer feedback on the draft RFP for what DoD is calling the Advancing AI Multiple Award Contract. The post Pentagon drafts large-scale government AI contract first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Revolutionizing HR and payroll: Strategies for a modern workforce
item.description: Learn how agencies are innovating their payroll services to meet evolving needs The post Revolutionizing HR and payroll: Strategies for a modern workforce first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Interpol clamps down on cybercrime and arrests over 1,000 suspects in Africa
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Interpol arrested 1,006 suspects in Africa during a massive two-month operation, clamping down on cybercrime that left tens of thousands of victims, including some who were trafficked, and produced millions in financial damages, the global police organization said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KRON4
